2605.13596.title: Creativity Bias: How Machine Evaluation Struggles with Creativity in Literary Translations
2605.13596.paper_summary: This article investigates the performance of automatic evaluation metrics (AEMs) and LLM-as-a-judge evaluation on literary translation across multiple languages, genres, and translation modalities. The aim is to assess how well these tools align with professionals when evaluating translation, creativity (creative shifts & errors), and see if they can substitute laborious manual annotations. A dataset of literary translations across three modalities (human translation, machine translation, and post-editing), three genres and three language pairs was created and annotated in detail for creativity by experienced professional literary translators. The results show that both AEMs and LLM-as-a-judge evaluations correlate poorly with professional evaluations on creativity, with LLM-as-a-judge showing a systematic bias in favour of machine-translated texts and penalising creative and culturally appropriate solutions. Moreover, performance is consistently worse for more literary genres such as poetry. This highlights fundamental limitations of current automatic evaluation tools for literary translation and the need to create new tools that do not frequently consider out of routine translations as errors.
2605.13596.paper_summary_zh: 這篇文章調查了自動評估指標（AEMs）和作為評審的LLM在多種語言、類型和翻譯模式下的文學翻譯表現。<lb/>其目的是評估這些工具在評價翻譯、創造力（創意轉變與錯誤）時與專業人士的對齊程度，以及它們是否能取代繁瑣的手動註解。<lb/>建立了一個涵蓋三種模式（人類翻譯、機器翻譯和後編輯）、三種類型和三對語言的文學翻譯數據集，並由經驗豐富的專業文學翻譯者詳細註解創造力。<lb/>結果顯示，AEMs和作為評審的LLM在創造力的專業評估中相關性較差，且作為評審的LLM在機器翻譯文本上顯示出系統性偏見，並懲罰創新和文化上合適的解決方案。<lb/>此外，對於更具文學性的類型，如詩歌，表現始終較差。<lb/>這突顯了當前自動評估工具在文學翻譯中的基本局限性，以及創建不經常將例外翻譯視為錯誤的新工具的必要性。
2605.13596.author: Kyo Gerrits et.al.
2605.13596.authors: Kyo Gerrits, Rik van Noord, Ana Guerberof Arenas
